full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Scaffolding data loading and preprocessing code
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: I need to set up the 20 Newsgroups dataset with all categories grouped into a binary classification — comp/sci/talk as +1 and the rest as -1. Use TfidfVectorizer with max_features=3000 and stratified 80/20 split. The paper (Chang et al., 2010) requires sparse data with l >> n_bar/2 per Section 3.3, so I need all categories to get around 15K training samples.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Produced the boilerplate code for fetch_20newsgroups, label mapping, and train_test_split.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I had already read Section 3.3 and Equation (10) to determine the dataset size and sparsity requirements. Confirmed the output shapes and class distribution matched my expectations.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Changed max_features from the initial 10000 to 3000 after I calculated the mapped dimension n + n(n+1)/2 would be ~50M at n=10000, causing OOM during polynomial mapping.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Generating sparsity analysis and visualization code
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Write code to compute n_bar (average nonzeros per row from indptr), n_hat approximation from Equation (10), and the per-iteration cost ratio (l * n_bar) / n_hat. Also plot a histogram of nonzeros per instance and a sparsity pattern heatmap for the first 100 rows. Save the figure to results/task_2_1_sparsity.png.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Generated the matplotlib plotting code and the np.diff(indptr) approach for computing row-wise nonzero counts.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I derived the n_hat formula and cost ratio from the paper myself. Verified the output values are consistent with the sparsity I observed in the raw data.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added the printed cost analysis section and the explicit check that n_bar/2 << l is satisfied, since that is the condition the paper states for the speedup to hold.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Getting ModuleNotFoundError for scipy when running the notebook — install scipy, scikit-learn, matplotlib, seaborn, pandas on this kernel.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Ran the package installation on the notebook kernel.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Re-ran all cells to confirm imports work.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — environment fix, no code changes.
